_default.name: Minimalist UI Flow and Progressive Disclosure

testCases.title: Authentication form should progressively disclose complex fields when user interacts with required fields
preconditions: User is on the authentication pop up (Sign In or Sign Up)

steps.content: Observe the initial state of the authentication form
steps.expected: Only essential fields (e.g., email and password) are visible

steps.content: Begin entering a valid email address in the email field
steps.expected: Email field accepts input and complex fields remain hidden

steps.content: Begin entering a password in the password field
steps.expected: Password field accepts input and any additional fields (e.g., password requirements, tooltips) are progressively disclosed as needed

testCases.expectedResult: Complex fields are hidden until user interaction triggers their disclosure, maintaining a minimalist UI

testCases.title: Authentication form should maintain minimalist design when user switches between Sign In and Sign Up
preconditions: User is on the authentication pop up

steps.content: Switch from Sign In to Sign Up
steps.expected: Form transitions smoothly and only essential fields for Sign Up are visible

steps.content: Switch back to Sign In
steps.expected: Form transitions smoothly and only essential fields for Sign In are visible

testCases.expectedResult: Minimalist design is preserved and transitions are seamless when switching between authentication states

_default.name: Inline Validation for Email and Password

testCases.title: Email field should display inline validation error when user enters invalid email format
preconditions: User is on the authentication pop up

steps.content: Enter 'invalid-email' (without @ symbol) in the email field
steps.expected: Email field accepts the input

steps.content: Observe the area directly beneath the email field
steps.expected: Inline validation error message is displayed indicating invalid email format

testCases.expectedResult: User sees inline validation error for invalid email format beneath the email field

testCases.title: Password field should display inline feedback for password strength when user enters password
preconditions: User is on the authentication pop up

steps.content: Enter 'weakpass' in the password field
steps.expected: Password field accepts input and masks characters

steps.content: Observe the area directly beneath the password field
steps.expected: Inline feedback is displayed indicating password strength (e.g., 'Weak password')

testCases.expectedResult: User sees real-time feedback for password strength beneath the password field

testCases.title: Password field should update inline feedback as password strength improves when user modifies password
preconditions: User is on the authentication pop up

steps.content: Enter 'weakpass' in the password field
steps.expected: Password field accepts input and displays 'Weak password' feedback

steps.content: Modify password to 'StrongPass123!'
steps.expected: Password field updates feedback to indicate improved strength (e.g., 'Strong password')

testCases.expectedResult: Inline feedback updates in real-time as password strength improves

testCases.title: Authentication form should display inline validation error when user attempts login with empty fields
preconditions: User is on the authentication pop up

steps.content: Leave email and password fields empty
steps.expected: Fields remain empty

steps.content: Click the 'Login' button
steps.expected: Inline validation errors are displayed beneath the empty fields

testCases.expectedResult: User sees inline validation errors for empty required fields and login is not performed

testCases.title: Password field should display contextual tooltip for password requirements when user focuses on password field
preconditions: User is on the authentication pop up

steps.content: Focus on the password field
steps.expected: Password field is focused

steps.content: Observe the area near the password field
steps.expected: Contextual tooltip appears displaying password requirements

testCases.expectedResult: User sees contextual tooltip for password requirements when password field is focused

testCases.title: Tooltip should disappear as password requirements are met when user enters compliant password
preconditions: User is on the authentication pop up

steps.content: Focus on the password field
steps.expected: Tooltip appears displaying password requirements

steps.content: Enter a password that meets all requirements (e.g., 'StrongPass123!')
steps.expected: Tooltip disappears as requirements are met

testCases.expectedResult: Tooltip disappears when all password requirements are satisfied

testCases.title: Tooltip should persist for unmet requirements when user enters partially compliant password
preconditions: User is on the authentication pop up

steps.content: Focus on the password field
steps.expected: Tooltip appears displaying password requirements

steps.content: Enter a password that meets some but not all requirements (e.g., 'StrongPass')
steps.expected: Tooltip persists, indicating which requirements are still unmet

testCases.expectedResult: Tooltip persists until all requirements are met, updating as requirements are satisfied

testCases.title: Authentication form should display smooth animation when user switches between Sign In and Sign Up
preconditions: User is on the authentication pop up

steps.content: Click the 'Sign Up' link/button from the Sign In form
steps.expected: Authentication form transitions with a smooth animation to Sign Up state

steps.content: Click the 'Sign In' link/button from the Sign Up form
steps.expected: Authentication form transitions with a smooth animation to Sign In state

testCases.expectedResult: User experiences smooth, seamless transitions between authentication states

testCases.title: Authentication form should maintain user orientation when user switches rapidly between Sign In and Sign Up
preconditions: User is on the authentication pop up

steps.content: Rapidly switch between Sign In and Sign Up forms multiple times
steps.expected: Authentication form transitions smoothly each time and maintains user orientation

testCases.expectedResult: User orientation is maintained and transitions remain seamless even with rapid switching

_default.name: Error Recovery and Forgot Password Flow

testCases.title: Authentication form should provide direct path to Forgot Password flow when login attempt fails
preconditions: User is on the authentication pop up

steps.content: Enter valid email and incorrect password in the login form
steps.expected: Fields accept input

steps.content: Click the 'Login' button
steps.expected: Login attempt fails and error message is displayed

steps.content: Observe the error recovery options
steps.expected: Direct path (e.g., link or button) to Forgot Password flow is visible

testCases.expectedResult: User sees direct path to Forgot Password flow after failed login attempt

testCases.title: Authentication form should allow user to access Forgot Password flow from any failed login attempt
preconditions: User is on the authentication pop up

steps.content: Attempt login with empty fields
steps.expected: Login attempt fails and error message is displayed

steps.content: Observe the error recovery options
steps.expected: Direct path to Forgot Password flow is visible

testCases.expectedResult: User can access Forgot Password flow from any failed login attempt

_default.name: Mobile UX: Thumb-Reachability and High-Contrast Buttons

testCases.title: Primary action button should be high-contrast and thumb-reachable for mobile users
preconditions: User is on the authentication pop up on a mobile device

steps.content: Observe the position and appearance of the primary action button (e.g., Login or Sign Up)
steps.expected: Button is positioned within thumb-reach and has high-contrast appearance

testCases.expectedResult: Primary action button is easily reachable and visually distinct for mobile users

testCases.title: Primary action button should not be thumb-reachable for desktop users
preconditions: User is on the authentication pop up on a desktop device

steps.content: Observe the position of the primary action button
steps.expected: Button is positioned for mouse interaction, not thumb-reach

testCases.expectedResult: Primary action button is not optimized for thumb-reach on desktop

_default.name: Alternative Login Methods: Social Login Separation and Branding

testCases.title: Social login buttons should be clearly separated from primary action button when user views authentication form
preconditions: User is on the authentication pop up

steps.content: Observe the layout of the authentication form
steps.expected: Social login buttons are visually separated from primary action button

testCases.expectedResult: Social login buttons are clearly separated from primary action button

testCases.title: Social login buttons should display recognizable brand logos when user views authentication form
preconditions: User is on the authentication pop up

steps.content: Observe the social login buttons
steps.expected: Each button displays a recognizable brand logo

testCases.expectedResult: Social login buttons display recognizable brand logos

testCases.title: Social login buttons should not display recognizable brand logos when branding is ambiguous
preconditions: User is on the authentication pop up

steps.content: Observe the social login buttons
steps.expected: Brand logos are missing or unclear

testCases.expectedResult: Ambiguity is surfaced if social login branding is not recognizable

_default.name: Backend: Real-Time Validation and Error Recovery

testCases.title: Backend should provide real-time validation feedback for email and password fields when user submits authentication data
preconditions: Authentication API is available

steps.content: Submit authentication request with invalid email format and weak password
steps.expected: API responds with validation errors for email and password

steps.content: Submit authentication request with valid email and strong password
steps.expected: API responds with success or appropriate authentication response

testCases.expectedResult: Backend provides real-time validation feedback for email and password fields

testCases.title: Backend should provide error recovery path for failed login attempts when user submits incorrect credentials
preconditions: Authentication API is available

steps.content: Submit authentication request with valid email and incorrect password
steps.expected: API responds with authentication failure and error recovery information (e.g., link to Forgot Password)

testCases.expectedResult: Backend provides error recovery path for failed login attempts
